architecture.summary: The paper evaluates Chain-of-Thought (COT) prompting applied to Large Language Models deployed on edge devices within wireless network environments. COT augments standard prompting by instructing the model to reason step-by-step before generating a response, with the mechanism flowing through text-based instruction in the prompt. The study measures how this reasoning strategy affects length control, compliance with maximum length constraints, and overall performance compared to standard prompting on two datasets.

biblio.title: Length Instruction Fine-Tuning with Chain-of-Thought (LIFT-COT): Enhancing Length Control and Reasoning in Edge-Deployed Large Language Models

claims.statement: COT reasoning significantly mitigates length bias and enhances output length control
COT strategy regulates response length and improves compliance with predefined maximum length (max_len) constraints

claims.dataset: two representative datasets (not named in abstract)
claims.statement: COT-augmented prompting demonstrates improved overall performance compared to standard prompting
